homeworkingByQuintile.note: Percentage working from home at least one day per week by household income quintile, UK, 2024. ONS HTUS.

nationalHomeworkingRate.note: Percentage of employed people who worked from home at least once per week, UK. ONS Labour Force Survey.

flexibilityRetention.note: Proportion of workers who say they would look for a new job if their employer removed flexible or remote working options.

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Homeworking and employer-employee working arrangements, UK

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Labour Force Survey — place of work

sources.name: CIPD
sources.dataset: Flexible and hybrid working practices survey

metadata.methodology: Homeworking by quintile from ONS Homeworking and Employer-Employee Working Arrangements survey. National homeworking rate from LFS. Retention survey data from CIPD / YouGov employer pulse surveys.
knownIssues: Quintile analysis reflects income rather than occupation — the two are correlated but not identical
2020 figures reflect mandatory lockdown homeworking rather than employer policy
Would-quit figures from survey data may overstate actual quit intentions
